content.full_text:
110 WEST C STREET, SUITE 1709
SAN DIEGO, CA 92101-3966
(619) 237-7844
FAX (619) 237-7229

1020 N STREET
SACRAMENTO, CA 95814-5691
(916) 445-5713
FAX (916) 324-3984

ERNEST J. DRONENBURG, JR.
MEMBER, STATE BOARD OF EQUALIZATION

April 4, 1991


Ms. Alice Hughes
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Ms. Hughes:

     This letter is to confirm our conversation of this morning and to confirm my understanding of the information and advice you gave.

     I serve as Deputy to the Honorable Ernest J. Dronenburg, Jr., Member of the State Board of Equalization.  Mr. Dronenburg, as a policy, does not accept honorarium when giving speeches. During one of the last reporting periods, Mr. Dronenburg made an address to the accountancy firm of Deloitte Touche. After he refused to accept any honorarium, the firm offered to give a donation to one of his favorite charities.  Mr. Dronenburg had me call back at a later date to accept that offer and suggested the Clair Burgener Foundation for the Developmentally Disabled. Neither Mr. Dronenburg nor I knew the dollar amount that was to be donated nor if the donation had in fact been made until several months later when Deloitte Touche was to come before the Board of Equalization for a hearing. Mr. Dronenburg at no time saw the check and never took any deduction on his income tax.

     My question to you was whether Mr. Dronenburg needed to report this as a gift or as income? You responded that it would be treated as non-reportable income as he did not mandate the donation and never exerted any control.  If this is an inaccurate understanding of our conversation would you please respond to me in writing as quickly as possible. If this is an accurate understanding, then I will interpret your silence as agreement.

     I thank you for your time and assistance.

Very Truly Yours,

Earl J. Santos, Jr.
Deputy to Board Member

[illegible]

A
